introSection: Thunder Dolphin is an Intamin hyper roller coaster located at Tokyo Dome City in Bunkyo, Tokyo, Japan. Thunder Dolphin's long course passes through both a hole in the LaQua building, as well as through the Big O, the world's first hubless ferris wheel. The ride opened in 2003 and is currently one of the park's two roller coasters. It is the tallest roller coaster to be classified as a hyper roller coaster (200 - 299 feet).

sections.title: Trains
sections.content:
5 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 20 riders per train.

The trains were replaced in spring 2023.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On 5 December 2010, a 25 cm bolt fell from the ride and injured a 9-year-old girl. Following this incident, Thunder Dolphin remained closed until 1 August 2013, nearly 3 years after the incident.

fullText:
Thunder Dolphin is an Intamin hyper roller coaster located at Tokyo Dome City in Bunkyo, Tokyo, Japan. Thunder Dolphin's long course passes through both a hole in the LaQua building, as well as through the Big O, the world's first hubless ferris wheel. The ride opened in 2003 and is currently one of the park's two roller coasters. It is the tallest roller coaster to be classified as a hyper roller coaster (200 - 299 feet).



5 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 20 riders per train.

The trains were replaced in spring 2023.

On 5 December 2010, a 25 cm bolt fell from the ride and injured a 9-year-old girl. Following this incident, Thunder Dolphin remained closed until 1 August 2013, nearly 3 years after the incident.
